front: How do you provision a VPC for a data platform with Terraform?
back:
A data platform VPC needs public subnets (load balancers), private subnets (compute/databases), and NAT gateways for outbound internet from private subnets.

Using the community VPC module (fastest path):
```hcl
module "vpc" {
  source  = "terraform-aws-modules/vpc/aws"
  version = "~> 5.0"

  name = "data-platform-prod"
  cidr = "10.0.0.0/16"

  azs             = ["us-east-1a", "us-east-1b", "us-east-1c"]
  private_subnets = ["10.0.1.0/24", "10.0.2.0/24", "10.0.3.0/24"]
  public_subnets  = ["10.0.101.0/24", "10.0.102.0/24", "10.0.103.0/24"]

  enable_nat_gateway   = true
  single_nat_gateway   = false  # one per AZ for HA
  enable_dns_hostnames = true

  # Tags required for EKS to find subnets:
  private_subnet_tags = {
    "kubernetes.io/role/internal-elb" = "1"
  }
  public_subnet_tags = {
    "kubernetes.io/role/elb" = "1"
  }
}
```

DE architecture:
- Private subnets: RDS, EMR, EKS nodes, Glue connections
- Public subnets: NAT gateways, bastion hosts, ALBs
- single_nat_gateway = true in dev (cheaper), false in prod (HA)
- 3 AZs minimum for production data platforms

S3 VPC endpoint (cost saving): add S3 gateway endpoint so EMR/Glue traffic to S3 does not go through NAT (free and faster).
